records.ety: [OE. almes, almesse, AS. ælmysse, fr. L. eleemosyna, Gr. 'elehmosy`nh mercy, charity, alms, fr. 'eleei^n to pity. Cf. Almonry, Eleemosynary.]

defs: Anything given gratuitously to relieve the poor, as money, food, or clothing; a gift of charity.
quotes: A devout man . . . which gave much alms to the people.
Alms are but the vehicles of prayer.
notes: ☞ This word alms is singular in its form (almesse), and is sometimes so used; as, “asked an alms.” Acts iii. 3. “Received an alms.” Shak. It is now, however, commonly a collective or plural noun. It is much used in composition, as almsgiver, almsgiving, alms bag, alms chest, etc.
